_provenance.labeler: Claude Opus 4.8 (claude-opus-4-8), in-session, no API key — the same benchmark-validated judge used for stance elsewhere in the pipeline
tasks: relevance: does the top-3 of each retrieval arm contain >=1 reference a domain reader would accept as genuinely about the claim? (the retrieval-quality anchor; NLI underdetects relevance on policy text, so the validated judge is used here exactly as it anchors stance elsewhere)

claude_stance: reading the abstract of the best on-topic reference in the Claude arm's top-3 (the re-ranking the single frontier model performs), does the retrieved science support / refute / stay silent on the claim? This yields an end-to-end corroboration read from a fully reproducible, no-second-API retrieval pipeline.
_provenance.fairness_note: Deliberately fair to the naive arm: one on-topic hit in the top 3 counts as relevant, and topically-adjacent hits (e.g. geothermal-potential papers with the wrong geography) are credited. 'corroboration' means the nearest literature is consistent with the claim's substance and direction, not that it verifies exact figures.
_provenance.ids: empirical-claim order 0-29 (0-14 Democratic, 15-29 Republican); the 20 non-empirical claims on which Claude abstained are not listed here

labels.note: Both retrieve heavy-truck platooning energy studies; abstract confirms fuel economy gains from reduced drag.
Climate-driven increases in wildfire danger corroborate elevated risk.
Early-childhood/teacher compensation-and-retention literature.
Naive off (implementation/diabetes); Claude re-ranks to 'Aging in Place vs Nursing Home Costs to Medicare/Medicaid' (rank-2), which supports cost reduction.
Claude retrieves biodiversity/species-richness papers (on-construct) but they do not substantiate the specific 7%/half proportion -> silent.
Basic-needs insecurity associated with worse academic achievement.

labels.claim: E-bikes can replace most car trips
labels.note: E-bike trip-substitution evidence supports car-trip replacement.
Both miss: a travel-survey statistic neither arm surfaces. Honest failure of the open retriever; no relevant ref to judge.
Claude retrieves criminal-justice-disparities papers (relevant) but the abstracts do not directly substantiate police under-identification -> silent (conservative).

labels.claim: Firearm deaths leading cause for children 1-19
labels.note: Naive: global mortality / life-expectancy forecasts (off). Claude: 'Major Causes of Death in Children/Adolescents US' and 'Trends in Firearm Deaths Among Children' -> support.
Naloxone-reversal pharmacology supports the antagonist mechanism.
Naive off (Alzheimer's/Kenya). Claude: former-foster-youth outcomes documenting housing instability -> support.
Homelessness-assistance evidence supports prevention via subsidy.

labels.claim: Youth mental health hit by COVID
labels.note: Naive off; Claude: COVID youth mental-health impact -> support.

labels.claim: Sea level to rise 1-3.4 ft by 2100
labels.note: Naive matched 'sea'/'climb' (sea stars, rock climbing). Claude: sea-level-rise projection literature -> support of multi-foot SLR by 2100.
Financial literacy linked to wealth/retirement-planning outcomes.
Socioeconomic disparities in literacy/knowledge corroborate unequal access (cautious).
Financial literacy linked to financial behavior.
Naive returned NOTHING; Claude: public-access defibrillation RCT showing improved OHCA survival -> support.
LLM-capability/agents literature describing rapid advance (cautious).
Naive off; Claude: telework can be effective / has well-being benefits, consistent with the claim (cautious).
Claude re-ranks to comparative respiratory-depression pharmacology (fentanyl vs heroin/morphine) -> support of far-higher potency.
Opioid-equivalence literature supports the order-of-magnitude potency ratio (cautious).
US opioid-overdose-mortality surveillance -> support.
Claude's top hits (hydrogen/fuel cells, geothermal brine) do not substantiate California geothermal magnitude -> silent.

labels.claim: Drowning leading death cause in autism
labels.note: Autism-specific drowning-mortality study -> support.
Claude retrieves EV-charging grid-impact papers (relevant) but they emphasize managing impacts, not an inability to cope -> silent (conservative).
Young adults lack basic financial knowledge -> supports need for early foundation.
Carbon-price incidence literature supports pass-through to consumer fuel cost (direction, not the exact 30c).
Retrieved LCFS paper concerns GHG reductions, not the specific 11c price impact -> silent.
